chief_complaint: A 77-year-old woman presented with a 3-week history of recurrent falls.
history: The patient experienced sudden unilateral knee buckling due to faciobrachio-crural dystonic seizures (FBCDS) while standing, leading to multiple contusions on her face and extremities. Brain MRI showed no abnormalities. Serum testing revealed positive leucine-rich glioma-inactivated 1 (LGI1) antibodies, confirming the diagnosis of LGI1 antibody-positive encephalitis.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Brain MRI: no abnormalities; Serum testing: positive LGI1 antibodies.
